source_quote: Human welfare and development is a basic right of every individual as enshrined in the constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan
claim: The PES Health chapter frames human welfare and health as a constitutional right, situating the discussion within a rights-based development discourse.

source_quote: SDGs Goal-3 and Goal-6 relates to health of people and under takes a commitment to end endemics of AIDS, TB, Malaria and other communicable disease by 2030
claim: Pakistan committed to SDG Goals 3 and 6, pledging to end AIDS, TB, and malaria epidemics by 2030.

source_quote: Pakistan has decided to translate SDG framework into National Development Planning. In order to operationalize SDGs, the federal government and four provincial governments have already committed to set up SDG Support Units
claim: The federal and four provincial governments established SDG Support Units to operationalize the SDG framework, with units set up in the Federal Planning Commission and Punjab.

source_quote: a National Health Program has been launched under the guidance of the Prime Minister by Ministry of National Health Services, Regulations and Coordination (MoNHSRC). The program will help millions of poor to access quality health care services
claim: The PM National Health Program was launched under MoNHSRC to provide universal health coverage, offering secondary care of Rs.50,000 and priority disease coverage of Rs.250,000 per family per year.
actor: Ministry of National Health Services, Regulations and Coordination
actors: Ministry of National Health Services, Regulations and Coordination

source_quote: in Pakistan only 2 polio cases has been reported this year till April 25th 2017 against 9 cases recorded during same period last year and the country is almost close to polio eradication
claim: Pakistan recorded only 2 polio cases by April 2017 down from 9 in the same period last year, indicating near-eradication.
effect: reduction in polio cases from 9 to 2

source_quote: the doctor population ratio stands at 1: 997, dentist 1: 10658 and hospital bed 1:1584. While national health infrastructure comprises of 1201 hospitals, BHUs 5518, RHCs 683
claim: Pakistan health infrastructure comprises 1,201 hospitals, 5,518 BHUs, and 683 RHCs, with a doctor-population ratio of 1:997 and one hospital bed per 1,584 persons.

source_quote: The country is spending 0.5 to 0.8 percent of its GDP on health over the last 10 years. These percentages are less than the WHO bench mark of at least 6 percent of GDP
claim: Pakistan health spending of 0.5-0.8% of GDP is far below the WHO benchmark of 6%, representing a structural financing gap undermining basic service delivery.
indicator_or_subject: health expenditure as percent of GDP

source_quote: During 2015-16, total expenditure increased by 13 percent over 2014-15, and during current fiscal year (JulyMarch) 2016-17, the expenditure remain at 145.97 billion showing an increase of 9 percent
claim: Total health expenditure reached Rs.145.97 billion in July-March 2016-17 (up 9% year-on-year), following a 13% increase in 2015-16.

subsection: 1. Family Planning and Primary Health Care (FP&PHC)
source_quote: The FP & PHC program so far has recruited more than 100,000 Lady Health Workers (LHWs). LHWs services encompasses the health conditions of women and children through improved sanitation, birth spacing, iron supplementation, larger vaccination coverage
claim: The FP&PHC program recruited over 100,000 Lady Health Workers delivering primary health services including sanitation, birth spacing, and immunization to women and children.
policy_or_programme: Family Planning and Primary Health Care Program
policy_or_programmes: Family Planning and Primary Health Care Program

source_quote: During 2015-16, 8 million children of 0-11 months and 6.5 million pregnant women were immunized against 7 deadly
claim: The EPI program immunized 8 million children (0-11 months) and 6.5 million pregnant women against seven vaccine-preventable diseases in 2015-16, with pentavalent vaccine introduced through UNICEF.

source_quote: The program targets is to reduce the malaria burden by 60% in high and moderate endemic districts/agencies and eliminate malaria in low endemic districts by the year 2017-18
claim: The Malaria Control Program targets 60% reduction in burden in high and moderate endemic districts, and elimination in low endemic districts, by 2017-18.

source_quote: Pakistan has the sixth highest burden of tuberculosis worldwide and is among the high multidrug-resistant tuberculosis burden countries. The government has declared tuberculosis a national emergency
claim: Pakistan ranks sixth globally for tuberculosis burden and is a high MDR-TB country; the government declared TB a national emergency and is implementing the DOTS strategy.
constraint_or_risk: sixth highest TB burden globally; MDR-TB country

source_quote: Incidence of TB stands at 231 per 100,000 population
claim: TB incidence in Pakistan stands at 231 per 100,000 population.

subsection: Prime Minister Program for Prevention and Control of Hepatitis in Pakistan
source_quote: The program also intends to decrease more than half of new cases of hepatitis B and C through
claim: The PM Hepatitis Program aims to halve new cases of hepatitis B and C through preventive interventions and subsidized treatment for patients who cannot afford the high cost of medicines.
indicator_or_subject: hepatitis B and C incidence reduction target
policy_or_programme: Prime Minister Program for Prevention and Control of Hepatitis
policy_or_programmes: Prime Minister Program for Prevention and Control of Hepatitis

source_quote: Currently, there are eighteen cancer hospitals functioning in different cities. These hospitals are equipped with latest and advanced facilities in Diagnostic/ Therapeutic Nuclear Medicine
claim: Pakistan Atomic Energy Commission operates 18 cancer hospitals across different cities equipped with diagnostic and therapeutic nuclear medicine facilities.

source_quote: The achievements of the health sector during 2016-17 included establishment of 7 Rural Health Centers (RHCs) 32 Basic Health Units (BHUs) and up gradation of 10 existing RHCs and 37 BHUs. The manpower included the addition of 4500 new doctors, 450 dentists, 3500 Nurses
claim: Health sector 2016-17 achievements: 7 new RHCs, 32 new BHUs, upgradation of 10 RHCs and 37 BHUs, plus 4,500 new doctors, 450 dentists, and 3,500 nurses added.

source_quote: Almost half of the population is suffering from high rates of malnutrition particularly children and mothers
claim: Almost half of Pakistan population suffers from malnutrition, with children and mothers disproportionately affected.
constraint_or_risk: widespread malnutrition affecting nearly half the population

source_quote: The caloric availability through major food items has remained at about 2485 calories a day per person
claim: Per capita caloric availability stands at approximately 2,485 calories per day, with food availability improving in 2016-17 through adjusted imports and exports.

source_quote: Federal Government has allocated Rs. 100 million in PSDP for the National Initiative for SDGs/Nutrition for next three years
claim: Federal government allocated Rs.100 million in the PSDP for the National Initiative for SDGs/Nutrition, with a PC-1 under preparation to deploy funds toward nutrition-sensitive activities over three years.

source_quote: Health profile of Pakistan is characterized by high population growth. Despite a significant investment in health sector over the year, the rate of progress is lower than most other countries. The disease pattern is heavily dominated by malnutrition and poor dietary practices
claim: Pakistan health progress lags behind most other countries despite significant investment, with disease burden dominated by malnutrition and poor dietary practices compounded by high population growth.
constraint_or_risk: progress lower than most other countries; disease dominated by malnutrition
cause: high population growth, poor dietary practices
